evidence: Compatibility facades are accumulating as permanent high-fan-in entrypoints instead of converging on one canonical surface (`handleError` aliasing `reportRuntimeError` in `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`, `getSupabaseClient` alias export in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`).

Overlay ownership is inconsistent across shared UI: some components require controlled visibility (`OverlayVisibilityState` in `src/shared/components/ModalContainer.tsx` and `src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx`), while others accept optional control then normalize internally (`OptionalOverlayVisibilityState` + `useOverlayVisibilityState` in `src/shared/components/MediaTypeFilter.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/InfoTooltip.tsx`).

Domain type boundaries are blurred by composing persistence and view-derived fields into a single widely shared row shape (`GenerationRow` in `src/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow.ts`) and then embedding that in other domain types (`images?: GenerationRow[]` in `src/domains/generation/types/shotViewRow.ts`).

Task payload contracts are modeled multiple times with near-identical field sets: `TravelPayloadCore` and `TravelBetweenImagesOrchestratorPayload` duplicate most keys, then payload is spread from `payloadCore` plus optional overlays (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`).

`individualTravelSegment.ts` builds overlapping payload objects (`buildOrchestratorDetails`, `buildTaskParamsPayload`, `buildIndividualSegmentParamsPayload`) repeating many of the same semantic fields (prompts, motion, lineage, model/resolution) across layers.

Video/image lightbox render-model hooks remap large state bags into other large state bags (slice builders + workflow props + layout props), increasing indirection with little transformation (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx`).

`generationTaskRepository.parsePrimaryTaskId` treats non-array `tasks` as `invalid_tasks_shape`, while `generationTransformers.extractTaskId` accepts both `string` and `string[]`, creating divergent interpretation of the same DB field.
`generationTaskCache` reduces repository mapping payload to `{ taskId, status }`, dropping `queryError` returned by `generationTaskRepository`, so failure context is lost at the cache seam.

Join-clips metadata initialization uses two different duration-loading boundaries: `clipInitService.getVideoDurationFromUrl` (raw `<video>` element, resolves `0` on error) vs `clipManager/metadata.loadClipDurationResult` (shared uploader extractor + structured failure result), producing inconsistent behavior and error semantics for similar operations.

Storage boundary usage is split across abstractions: `pages/Home/hooks/auth/storage.ts` uses storage contracts exclusively, while `clipInitService.ts` mixes contract reads with direct local-storage-safe helpers and ad-hoc key handling, increasing seam entropy.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` contains unresolved merge-conflict markers (`<<<<<<<`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>`) inside the task builder flow, which is a direct code-integrity defect.
The same function block in `createIndividualTravelSegmentTask` now contains duplicated resolution-derivation logic paths around the conflict region, making control flow ambiguous and fragile.
Adjacent test module (`src/shared/lib/tasks/__tests__/individualTravelSegment.test.ts`) exercises behavior but does not guard against this source-integrity failure mode.

findings.summary: Back-compat facades remain first-class APIs, obscuring canonical ownership boundaries.
evidence: `handleError` is kept as an alias wrapper over `reportRuntimeError`, preserving two public names for one responsibility.
`getSupabaseClient` is only a renamed re-export of `getSupabaseRuntimeClient`, adding another public entrypoint without behavioral distinction.
The mobile hook barrel (`index.ts`) continues to centralize cross-cutting exports, increasing dependency fan-in around alias surfaces.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical export name per responsibility, migrate call sites with a codemod, then remove alias exports on a scheduled deprecation date; enforce via lint rule or import restriction so new code cannot reintroduce legacy entrypoints.

findings.summary: Shared overlay components use conflicting visibility-control contracts across the same UI layer.
evidence: `ModalContainer` requires `open`/`onOpenChange` (`OverlayVisibilityState`), while `MediaTypeFilter` and `InfoTooltip` accept optional control and synthesize behavior via helper hooks.
`InstallInstructionsModal` and `DescriptionModal` are hard-controlled wrappers over `ModalContainer`, but neighboring components in the same shared/UI area use optional contracts.
This mixed model makes ownership of overlay state non-obvious and increases ripple risk when changing overlay lifecycle conventions.
findings.suggestion: Standardize shared overlay APIs on a single controllable pattern (explicit controlled props plus one shared controllable-state helper for optional mode), then align all overlay-capable shared components to that contract.

findings.summary: Core generation types conflate persisted and view-derived concerns, widening coupling across domain models.
evidence: `GenerationRow` is defined as `PersistedGenerationRow & GenerationDerivedFields`, so display-layer derivations become part of the domain row contract.
`ShotViewModel.images` depends on `GenerationRow[]`, propagating the mixed persisted+derived shape into adjacent domain types.
findings.suggestion: Split persisted domain entities from view-model projections (e.g., keep `PersistedGenerationRow` as canonical domain type and expose derived/UI projections from mapper/selectors), then update shot-facing types to depend on explicit projection types only where needed.

findings.summary: Unresolved merge-conflict markers remain in production task builder code
evidence: `createIndividualTravelSegmentTask` includes literal conflict markers around resolution/task param assembly (`<<<<<<< Updated upstream`, `=======`, `>>>>>>> Stashed changes`).
Both branches include overlapping resolution logic, so the intended final control path is not cleanly represented.
findings.suggestion: Resolve the conflict explicitly by keeping a single resolution derivation block and a single `taskParams = buildIndividualTravelSegmentParams(...)` path, then add a focused test asserting the resolved branch behavior (resolution precedence + payload creation) so this cannot regress silently.

findings.summary: Task payload abstractions duplicate schema definitions across adjacent layers
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesOrchestratorPayload` restates most of `TravelPayloadCore` fields while also embedding `task_payload_contract` and then spreading `payloadCore` into final payload.
`individualTravelSegment.ts` separately builds orchestrator details, top-level params, and nested `individual_segment_params` with repeated keys and similar derivation logic.
`joinClips.ts` follows another custom payload-contract assembly path rather than sharing a common task-family payload adapter.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one canonical payload schema composer per task family (core + overlays), and derive transport shapes from that single source (e.g., typed builder returning `{core, contracts, optional}`) to remove repeated field declarations and reduce schema drift.

findings.summary: Lightbox integration seams rely on large pass-through prop/state bags
evidence: `ImageLightbox` and `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` map many fields from env/shared/edit models into new objects (slices, workflow props, button-group props) with limited transformation.
`LightboxLayout` consumes a wide `LightboxLayoutProps` surface and multiple context slices, coupling rendering to many upstream orchestration details.
Any change to shot workflow/actions/features tends to require synchronized edits across multiple handoff builders.
findings.suggestion: Collapse the seam into a smaller explicit boundary object (e.g., `lightboxCapabilities` + `lightboxState`) and move field translation to one adapter per mode, so layout/components depend on stable minimal contracts rather than raw upstream bags.

findings.summary: UI orchestrator components fan out too many cross-module responsibilities
evidence: `ShotImageManagerContainer` composes many manager hooks and passes broad manager objects/callback sets into downstream components.
`JoinClipsPage` similarly coordinates settings, clip manager, generation, DnD, gallery, and deletion flows in one boundary layer and forwards many handlers to `SortableClip`/`MediaGallery`.
`MediaGallery` accepts a very wide prop surface and internally coordinates many sub-hooks, increasing caller-to-callee seam complexity.
findings.suggestion: Split orchestration boundaries by workflow (selection, ordering, generation, rendering) and introduce smaller typed controller interfaces per child component so changes stay localized and cross-module handoffs become predictable.

findings.summary: Task mapping and generation transformation disagree on accepted `tasks` payload shape.
evidence: `parsePrimaryTaskId` returns `invalid_tasks_shape` when `tasks` is not an array of strings.
`extractTaskId` accepts `tasks` as `string | string[] | null` and treats a single string as valid task id.
findings.suggestion: Define a single shared decoder for generation `tasks` payload (e.g., in a shared contract module) and use it in both repository and transformers so status reporting and UI extraction follow one contract.

findings.summary: Generation-task cache drops query diagnostics from repository results at the handoff.
evidence: `GenerationTaskMapping` includes `queryError` for `query_failed` status.
`GenerationTaskCacheEntry` only stores `taskId` and `status`, so downstream consumers cannot inspect why mapping failed.
findings.suggestion: Extend `GenerationTaskCacheEntry` to include optional `queryError` (or structured failure info) and preserve it when caching mappings to keep the boundary lossless.

findings.summary: Join-clips uses two incompatible metadata-loading seams for clip duration.
evidence: `clipInitService` computes duration via a local `<video>` metadata probe and resolves `0` on errors.
`clipManager/metadata` computes duration via `extractVideoMetadataFromUrl` and returns structured `OperationResult` with explicit failure metadata.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate duration loading behind one shared service that returns a single result contract, then reuse it from both pending-clip consumption and clip manager metadata loading paths.

findings.summary: Storage integration alternates between contract-based and ad-hoc APIs across adjacent modules.
evidence: Auth storage wrapper consistently delegates reads/writes/clears through `storageContract` APIs.
Join-clips initialization mixes `readStorageContract` with direct `readLocalStorageItem`/`writeLocalStorageItem` and manual key shaping.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one storage boundary for feature code (prefer contract-based decoding/migration), and move ad-hoc localStorage key access behind shared contract helpers.
